From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Rhythm Dhol Bass](/w/index.php?title=Rhythm_Dhol_Bass&redirect=no "Rhythm Dhol Bass"))

British band, formed 1997

| RDB Rhythm Dhol Bass | |
| --- | --- |
| Origin | [Bradford](/wiki/Bradford "Bradford"), West Yorkshire, England |
| Genres | [Bhangra](/wiki/Bhangra_(music) "Bhangra (music)") [Hip hop](/wiki/Hip_hop_music "Hip hop music") [Bollywood](/wiki/Bollywood_music "Bollywood music") |
| Years active | 1997– 2014 |
| Label | Three Records ltd |
|  | |
| Members | Surjeet Singh Ral (SurjRDB) |
|  | |
| Past members | [Manjeet Singh Ral](/wiki/Manjeet_Singh_Ral "Manjeet Singh Ral") (Manj Musik) and Kuldeep Singh Ral |
|  | |
| Website | [www.rhythmdholbass.com](http://www.rhythmdholbass.com/) |

**RDB** (an acronym for **[Rhythm](/wiki/Rhythm "Rhythm"), [Dhol](/wiki/Dhol "Dhol"), [Bass](/wiki/Bass_(sound) "Bass (sound)")**) is a band initially formed in 1997 by three [British Indian](/wiki/British_Indian "British Indian") brothers, Kuldeep, Surjeet and [Manjeet Singh Ral](/wiki/Manjeet_Singh_Ral "Manjeet Singh Ral"). The band's style blends western genres with traditional Punjabi beats and vocals.[[1]](#cite_note-1) After the death of the eldest brother Kuldeep in 2012 and the departure of Manjeet to start a solo career, RDB has continued with the brother Surjeet.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=RDB_(band)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

RDB's musical journey began with singing at the [gurdwara](/wiki/Gurdwara "Gurdwara"): "We used to assist our father in performing in front of the community at our local gurdwara, playing the [harmonium](/wiki/Pump_organ "Pump organ") and the [tabla](/wiki/Tabla "Tabla"). This gave us a great understanding of our musical creativity and we mixed this with our passion for technology and started experimenting with sounds." In 2003, they won "Best Club DJ Bhangra" at the [UK Asian Music Awards](/wiki/UK_Asian_Music_Awards "UK Asian Music Awards").[[2]](#cite_note-2005brlaunch-2) In April 2011, Kuldeep was diagnosed with a brain tumour and underwent [radiotherapy](/wiki/Radiotherapy "Radiotherapy") and [chemotherapy](/wiki/Chemotherapy "Chemotherapy").[[3]](#cite_note-death-3) He died on 22 May 2012 in [Houston](/wiki/Houston "Houston"), Texas.[[3]](#cite_note-death-3)

While originally the remaining brothers planned to continue working together,[[4]](#cite_note-4) A few months after Kuldeep's death, the band split and Surjeet and Manjeet went their separate ways. Surjeet continued to perform under the RDB banner, while Manjeet operated as an independent artist.[[5]](#cite_note-lawsuit-5) In 2013 RDB was awarded Best Music Video for "We Doin’ It Big" at the [Brit Asia TV Music Awards](/wiki/Brit_Asia_TV_Music_Awards "Brit Asia TV Music Awards") (BAMA).[[6]](#cite_note-BAMA2013-6) In BAMA 2014 RDB was awarded Best Music Video for "Daddy Da Cash" feat. [T-Pain](/wiki/T-Pain "T-Pain").[[7]](#cite_note-BAMA2014-7)

In 2014 Manjeet filed a lawsuit against Surjeet over the use of RDB songs and brand.[[5]](#cite_note-lawsuit-5) An application for an [interim injunction](/wiki/Interim_injunction "Interim injunction") was rejected by the [Bombay High Court](/wiki/Bombay_High_Court "Bombay High Court").[[8]](#cite_note-8)
According to Surjeet, the a lawsuit was withdrew in 2016.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
As of 2019[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=RDB_(band)&action=edit), Manjeet is operating independently.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
